why: why use modules instead of script tags
how: how to use modules in browser
output: cors error when opening module from file protocol

1_beginner_explanation.summary: As your JavaScript application grows, putting 10,000 lines of code into a single `app.js` file becomes a nightmare to manage. A **Module** is just a separate JavaScript file that contains a small, specific piece of code (like a single Class or a few math functions). Modules allow you to break your app into tiny, organized files that can share data with each other.
1_beginner_explanation.analogy: Imagine building a Lego castle. A non-module app is like having all 5,000 pieces dumped into one giant bucket; it takes forever to find what you need. Modules are like sorting the pieces into separate labeled bags (Roof Bag, Wall Bag, Door Bag). It makes building and finding things infinitely easier.

2_internal_mechanism.execution_flow: When the browser sees `<script type="module" src="app.js">`, it kicks off a Module Graph. It scans `app.js` for `import` statements, downloads those files asynchronously, parses them, maps out all dependencies, and links them in memory BEFORE executing any code.
2_internal_mechanism.memory: Unlike old `<script>` tags which dumped everything into the global `window` object (causing naming collisions), every Module has its own completely isolated memory scope.

3_real_world_example.use_case: Splitting a large application into manageable files.
3_real_world_example.code:
<!-- index.html -->
<!-- You MUST add type="module" to enable the module system -->
<script type="module" src="main.js"></script>

/* 
File Structure:
- main.js (Entry point)
- auth.js (Handles login)
- ui.js (Handles buttons)

Instead of one file, auth.js and ui.js export their functions,
and main.js imports them to assemble the final application.
*/

4_common_confusion.trap: Trying to open `index.html` directly from your desktop (`file:///C:/index.html`) and wondering why your modules fail with a CORS error. Browsers strictly block ES Modules from running on the `file://` protocol for security reasons.
4_common_confusion.fix: You MUST run a local development server (like VS Code Live Server or `npx serve`) to use ES Modules.

6_edge_case.quirk: ES Modules automatically and permanently run in Strict Mode (`use strict`). You do not need to declare it at the top of the file, and you cannot turn it off.
